steps.assistant_text: RSP/RDP-style command emit (kernel_022_d, -O1, 0x84 / 33 insns): a0 carries a 6-bit signed field x, a1 a 5-bit signed field y. They are packed into a 4-byte stack command word (byte0 bits 2-7 = x<<2, byte1 bits 0-4 = y), then func_80006A50(0x04001000, word) submits the word and func_80006A50(0x04080000, 0) kicks/commits. Disassembled the target from expected/src/kernel/kernel_022_d.c.o (objdump -dr). The prior NM wrap was recorded as a ~68% structure-driven cap with char bitfields. Two levers cracked it byte-exact: (1) use INT bitfields, not char -- a char/signed-char bitfield forces an 8-bit sign-truncation (sll 0x18; sra 0x18) on the assigned value before the field shift, which the target does NOT have; int bitfields store the already-6-bit sign-extended value directly (target's plain sll<<2; andi 0xfc). (2) route the word zero-init and the *(int*) read through a union { struct bits; s32 w; } overlay -- a bare struct with *(int*)&cmd reads (or a u8[4] array) is address-taken at -O1 (addiu rX,sp; sw zero,0(rX) ... lw a1,0(rX)), whereas union.w keeps it a direct frame slot (single sw zero,28(sp); direct lb/sb RMW; lw a1,28(sp)). The byte read-backs survive as andi 0xff03 / 0xffe0 (complement masks promoted to int, low-16 andi imm). build/.o vs expected/.o: 33 insns / 0x84 bytes byte-identical. Full make clean && make ROM byte-identical to baserom (cmp tenshoe.z64 baserom.z64 clean; sha1 79cd1166c365e5809dec9b62e6d40d6032d5db3a; make verify: ROM OK). Kernel link-direct, real symbols.

metadata.verification: build/.o vs expected/.o func region 0x9C30..0x9CB4: 33 insns (0x84 bytes) byte-identical; full make clean && make ROM byte-identical to baserom (cmp tenshoe.z64 baserom.z64 clean; sha1 79cd1166c365e5809dec9b62e6d40d6032d5db3a; make verify OK). Cracked the old ~68% char-bitfield cap via int bitfields (kills 8-bit sign-truncation) + union overlay (kills address-taken init/read at -O1).
